Badge

Stable

Small status indicators for counts, labels, tags, and states. Supports variants, sizes, dot indicators, and removable tags.

Overview

The Badge component is used for status indicators, labels, tags, counts, and other small pieces of metadata. It supports six color variants, two sizes, a dot indicator mode, and removable tag functionality. Badges are fully accessible with proper ARIA attributes.

Live Demo

Interact with the Badge component — try clicking the X buttons on removable tags.

Variants

DefaultPrimarySuccessWarningDangerNeutral

Sizes

SmallMedium

Dot Indicators

OnlineAwayOffline

Removable Tags (click X to remove)

TypeScriptReactTailwindAstro

Variants

Six visual variants map to different semantic meanings.

Default Primary Success Warning Danger Neutral
badge-variants.tsx 
import { Badge } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function BadgeVariants() {
  return (
    <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2">
      <Badge variant="default">Default</Badge>
      <Badge variant="primary">Primary</Badge>
      <Badge variant="success">Success</Badge>
      <Badge variant="warning">Warning</Badge>
      <Badge variant="danger">Danger</Badge>
      <Badge variant="neutral">Neutral</Badge>
    </div>
  );
}

Sizes

Small Medium
badge-sizes.tsx 
import { Badge } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function BadgeSizes() {
  return (
    <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2">
      <Badge size="sm">Small</Badge>
      <Badge size="md">Medium</Badge>
    </div>
  );
}

Dot Indicator

Add dot to show a small colored circle before the text — useful for live status indicators.

Online Away Offline
badge-dot.tsx 
import { Badge } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function BadgeDot() {
  return (
    <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2">
      <Badge variant="success" dot>Online</Badge>
      <Badge variant="warning" dot>Away</Badge>
      <Badge variant="danger" dot>Offline</Badge>
    </div>
  );
}

Removable Tags

Use removable with an onRemove callback for tag-like badges that can be dismissed.

badge-removable.tsx 
import { Badge } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function BadgeRemovable() {
  const [tags, setTags] = React.useState(['React', 'TypeScript', 'Design']);

  return (
    <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2">
      {tags.map((tag) => (
        <Badge
          key={tag}
          variant="primary"
          removable
          onRemove={() => setTags((prev) => prev.filter((t) => t !== tag))}
        >
          {tag}
        </Badge>
      ))}
    </div>
  );
}

Edge Cases & Error States

Very Long Badge Text

Badges do not wrap text. Long content will stretch the badge horizontally. For text longer than 20 characters, consider using a different UI treatment or truncating via className with max-w-[160px] truncate.

badge-truncated.tsx 
import { Badge } from '@storyhouse/components';

export function BadgeTruncated() {
  return (
    <Badge className="max-w-[160px] truncate">
      Very long badge text that should be truncated
    </Badge>
  );
}

Multiple Removable Badges — Keyboard Navigation

Each removable badge has an X button that is a focusable <button> element. Users can Tab through multiple remove buttons. The remove button has aria-label="Remove" for screen reader clarity. Ensure onRemove handlers manage focus correctly — move focus to the next remaining badge after removal.

Small Size with Dot

The dot indicator works in both sm and md sizes. The dot remains the same 6px diameter regardless of badge size, which keeps it visually consistent across sizes.

Accessibility of Dynamic Badges

When badges represent dynamic content (e.g., live status updates), consider wrapping the container with aria-live="polite" or role="status" so screen readers announce changes. Badges themselves don't have built-in live region support because they don't know the context in which they're used.

Best Practices & Usage Guidelines

Semantic Variant Selection

  • Default — Generic labels with no semantic association
  • Primary — Feature flags, category labels, selected states
  • Success — Completed actions, active status, available resources
  • Warning — Pending actions, expiring items, moderate issues
  • Danger — Errors, blocked states, failed actions
  • Neutral — Counts, metadata, secondary information

When to Use Dot

The dot prop is ideal for real-time status indicators: online/offline presence, live/unprocessed notifications, active/inactive states. The dot color always matches the variant color for visual coherence. Do not use the dot for static labels — it implies liveness.

Layout Considerations

  • Use flex flex-wrap gap-2 for badge groups — badges wrap naturally on small screens
  • Avoid more than 6-8 badges in a single row — visual overload
  • Place badges near the content they annotate (after titles, next to metrics)
  • Removable badges should have an accessible remove confirmation if the action is destructive
  • Use sm size inside dense data tables and list items

Accessibility

  • Badge text is directly readable by assistive technology — no ARIA hiding needed
  • Remove button has aria-label="Remove" — screen readers announce it clearly
  • Dot indicator uses aria-hidden="true" to prevent screen reader distraction
  • All color variants maintain WCAG AA contrast (verified against white backgrounds)
  • Color is not the only indicator of meaning — always pair with text

Implementation Details

Dot Color Architecture

The dot indicator color is determined by a dotColors map that mirrors the variantStyles map. This ensures the dot always matches the badge text color scheme. The dot is rendered as a 6px rounded span with aria-hidden="true".

Removable Button Integration

The remove button is a native <button> element inside the badge span, making it keyboard-focusable. It uses a small inline SVG X icon and a hover state with semi-transparent background (hover:bg-black/5). The onRemove callback fires on click — the component does not manage its own state.

Dark Mode

All variant background colors have dark mode counterparts via the dark: prefix. For example, bg-green-100 becomes dark:bg-green-950. Text colors shift from bright to -300 shade for readability on dark backgrounds.

Props

Prop Type Default Description
variant "default" | "primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "neutral" "default" Semantic color variant for different contexts.
size "sm" | "md" "md" Badge size. Use sm for compact layouts.
dot boolean false Shows a small colored circle indicator before the text.
removable boolean false Shows an X button for dismissing the badge.
onRemove () => void - Callback fired when the remove button is clicked.
children React.ReactNode - Content inside the badge.
className string - Additional CSS classes.

Accessibility

  • Remove button has aria-label="Remove" for screen readers
  • Dot indicator uses aria-hidden="true" to prevent screen reader distraction
  • Badge text is directly readable by assistive technology
  • Color variants maintain sufficient contrast ratio (all pass WCAG AA)
